steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence names two cities — Quito, Ecuador, and Kisumu, Kenya. The second sentence says 'In other words' and restates the same idea, so the blank must refer to both cities. That means we need a plural demonstrative that agrees with the plural noun 'cities.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — these: plural demonstrative, correctly modifies the plural noun 'cities' and refers back to both cities. ✓
  • Choice B — each: always singular; does not agree with the plural noun 'cities.' ✗
  • Choice C — that: singular demonstrative; does not agree with the plural noun 'cities.' ✗
  • Choice D — this: singular demonstrative; does not agree with the plural noun 'cities.' ✗
A is correct.
